frame_label: 'Not All Men' Dismissal & Why Women Fear
generated_comment: These days whenever women discuss safety, it sounds like all men are being treated as suspects. I have daughters also, so I understand concern, but every decent man should not be made to feel guilty or untrustworthy for what some criminals do. Men also face problems outside, but nobody talks about that.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it dismisses women's fear by centering men's hurt feelings and demanding trust for 'decent men.' It reflects the 'not all men' response and the deflection to men's safety instead of engaging with structural reasons for women's wariness.
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 48. Married. A man in a semi-urban town in Karnataka. Your daughter Lakshmi who is 23 just got a job offer in Bengaluru at a good company. You are proud of her. But a marriage proposal has also just come in from a family you know and respect. The boy is 28, settled, good family. Your wife thinks they should accept the proposal. You are not sure because you are thinking about Lakshmi's skin colour Ñ she is very dark Ñ and what that will mean for future proposals if this one falls through and whether a good proposal at 23 for a dark skinned girl is something you can afford to let go even if the timing is not perfect for Lakshmi.
